sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 6g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 6g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 60.7g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for legumes, the carb story matters less when fibre and protein are both backing it up.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 60.7g. the fibre support does a lot of the heavy lifting. for legumes, the carb story matters less when fibre and protein are both backing it up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 19.3g. essential amino acids score is where the section starts to wobble. legumes need protein support to feel worth the carbs.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 19.3g. essential amino acids score is where the section starts to wobble. legumes need protein support to feel worth the carbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 4% DV, vitamin c at 7% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 4% DV, vitamin c at 7% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 34% DV, magnesium at 29% DV, zinc at 27% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 34% DV, magnesium at 29% DV, zinc at 27% DV stand out most. that is useful, but still not a huge mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. strong all-round legume profile because fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food. easy to use across lots of meals because works in bowls, curries, salads, hummus, and snacks. pretty filling and steady because useful as a practical staple rather than just a niche health food.

displayItems.explanation: Fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side. strong all-round legume profile because fibre, minerals, and useful protein all show up well enough to make it a very solid category food. easy to use across lots of meals because works in bowls, curries, salads, hummus, and snacks. pretty filling and steady because useful as a practical staple rather than just a niche health food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. sugar is a bit high for the category because not a disaster, but it is less clean than the best legumes on this axis. can be rough on some people digestively because legume tolerance varies a lot. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

closing.summary: Chickpeas stands out for strong all-round legume profile and easy to use across lots of meals. but it also gets held back by sugar is a bit high for the category and can be rough on some people digestively. Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
